tweets.text:
Local law enforcement in California accused Aman Kumar, of India, of striking a 4-year-old boy with his vehicle and fleeing the scene of the accident. The boy is expected to survive but tragically sustained critical injuries.

Due to sanctuary policies, the state released 30-year-old Kumar from jail despite knowing he is an illegal alien facing felony hit-and-run charges.

Kumar is currently in removal proceedings, so ICE had to assemble a larger team and apprehend him in the community because California jails refuse to work with ICE.

🔎Currently, more than 33,000 illegal immigrants in California custody have active detainer requests tied to crimes ranging from homicide and assault to robbery, drug offenses, weapons crimes, and sexual predatory offenses.

tweets.text:
ICE’s first interaction with Alejandro Paulino-Espidio was in 2006, when he was found among 17 illegal aliens hiding in the back of a van. He was granted voluntary return to Mexico, but that wasn’t the last ICE would see of him.

He snuck back into the United States by climbing a border fence in 2018. When Border Patrol agents found him, he claimed he planned to live with some friends in Orange County, California. Federal officials deported him that time.

But he returned illegally — again. 

Then, in 2022, a California judge in Fresno County convicted him of arson. A few years later, a California judge in Merced County convicted him of escaping from custody and vandalism.

➡️ICE deported Paulino-Espidio May 16. His repeated disregard of Americans boundaries, laws and safety shows clearly why @DHSgov has prioritized immigration enforcement and removal AND border security.

Five defendants pleaded guilty to federal firearms offenses while a federal grand jury indicted eight others in an international firearms trafficking conspiracy that funneled illegally obtained weapons from New Hampshire through the Akwesasne Mohawk Indian Reservation in New York.

ICE @HSIBuffalo, NY, and @ATFHQ led the investigation that identified about 50 firearms potentially trafficked from New Hampshire to Canada through Vermont and New York, several of which were recovered at various crime scenes, to include kidnapping and attempted murder, in Canada.

tweets.text:
An ICE HSI @HSIKansasCity probe ended with a 50-year sentence for Missouri man Richard James Miller for recording himself sexually abusing children over several years.

One victim said his abuse began when she was 15. The other said she was eight or nine years old.

Both said Miller threatened to kill their relatives, and one said he threatened to make her watch him kill her two dogs.

Details: https://t.co/D3hw0PXWYF 

This case is part of Project Safe Childhood, a nationwide Department of Justice initiative to combat the growing epidemic of child sexual exploitation and abuse. Learn more at https://t.co/XpRN94Mz9o.

Henry Roldan Perez-Roblero, 21, of Guatemala, faces a felony charge in Florida for allegedly sexually assaulting a child under 12 — a capital felony punishable in the state by death.

ICE issued a detainer with law enforcement in Martin County, Florida, and will take custody of him in the event he is ever released. 

👉Unlike sanctuary jurisdiction officials, Florida officials honor our detainers.

Perez-Roblero illegally entered the United States in Arizona in January 2021 and was released into the country. An immigration judge ordered him removed January 22, 2025.

Miguel Solis-Martinez, of Honduras, was arrested in 2008 for soliciting someone to murder his spouse in Bexar County, Texas. He was sentenced to a decade behind bars.

🔎Solis snuck into the country in 1999 near San Ysidro, Calif., and was put into removal proceedings. An immigration judge granted him voluntary departure in 2002 — in other words, he agreed to leave the country on his own rather than get deported. He didn’t leave though.

In addition to the homicide-related conviction, his rap sheet also includes:
➡️Illegal re-entry conviction
➡️Forgery charge
➡️DUI arrest

tweets.text:
Nhan Tu Hoang, of Vietnam, killed Kathy Brown Arceneaux, at her home Feb. 29, 1996.

⚖️Court documents tell the horrific, tragic story:

➡️Hoang and some friends had been out drinking and decided to rob houses. They grabbed a rifle and Hoang proceeded to fire shots at a house. When dogs began barking, Hoang and his friend shot at the animals, then left the scene to reload the firearm.  

➡️Hoang testified that when he later returned to scene, he was startled by a woman leaning over her dog and started shooting.

➡️Hoang shot Arceneaux five times — killing her. 

He was convicted and sentenced to prison. ICE arrested him May 5 upon his release and will hold him pending deportation.

Zakaria Songotoua was convicted in absentia and sentenced to 30 years for murder, assault and battery after he fled Burkina Faso before trial. 

As a member of the Presidential Security Regiment, he participated in the attempted 2015 coup d’etat in the African nation that included killing 11 and holding the interim president hostage.

After hiding in the sanctuary city of Bronx, N.Y., our New York officers and special agents tracked him down and returned him to Burkina Faso to face justice.

The U.S. is no safe haven for war criminals, and ICE HSI's Human Rights Violators and War Crimes Center ensures those who engage in such bloodshed anywhere in the world have no place in the United States.

tweets.text:
Oscar Alberto Pelaez was born in Colombia and immigrated to the U.S. to serve as a priest in California.

➡️1999 | Pelaez submitted an application for naturalization, claiming he never committed a crime for which he had not been arrested. 

➡️2001 | Pelaez became a U.S. citizen. 

➡️2002 | Pelaez was convicted of 13 felony offenses after confessing to repeatedly raping a 14-year-old boy in his parish between 1998 and 2000. 

Thanks to an ICE-initiated case through Operation False Haven, the Department of Justice filed a civil complaint to revoke Pelaez’s citizenship for concealing these crimes while applying for naturalization.

Cristel Daniela Cruz-Banegas climbed over the border fence and snuck into the United States in 2018 just months before she turned 18. 

🔎At that time, she told Border Patrol that she travelled with some friends from her home nation of Honduras to the U.S. by bus, boat, train and car. She said she lost contact with her friends in Sinaloa, Mexico, and made the rest of the journey herself. 

By 2025, she had been convicted of trafficking fentanyl. ICE took custody of her upon her release from federal prison May 1.

tweets.text:
Noslan Ruiz-Bernal, of Cuba, was convicted of armed burglary in 2014 right after receiving his green card. He served just over a year in jail  and was released back into the community after his incarceration.

More recently, he was arrested for armed  burglary again, but this time by Broward County, Fla., law enforcement — a 287(g) partner. 

Broward County honored ICE’s detainer and handed him over to federal officers rather than releasing back into the community to reoffend — something sanctuary jurisdictions are unwilling to do.

💻Learn more about the 287(g) program and ICE detainers on our website.

tweets.text:
Soili Xiomara Aparicio-Santos, of Honduras, was convicted in Suffolk County, N.Y., of trying to smother her newborn son to death with a pillow immediately after giving birth.

A jury convicted her in 2018, and she was facing a 16-year prison sentence. Later, an appellate judge in New York upheld her guilty conviction but reduced her sentence to just 10 years.

ICE arrested her upon her release from the Taconic Correctional Facility in Bedford Hills, N.Y., April 6 and deported her April 15.

🔎She entered the United States at an unknown place and time over a decade ago and had no legal right to remain this country. An immigration judge ordered her removed in 2014.

tweets.text:
Las Vegas police arrested John Young Cotter Johnstone, an illegal alien from the UK, who police say was captured on video dragging and swinging dogs by their leashes and kicking one dog.

Johnstone faces multiple charges connected to animal abuse he allegedly committed while working at a local rescue in Nevada.

The same day Johnstone was arrested, ICE issued an immigration detainer with the Clark County Detention Center. The next day, April 2, Clark County officials honored the detainer, and ICE took custody of the alleged animal abuser.

Johnstone came to the United States under the Visa Waiver Program as a visitor. He was supposed to leave the country by Feb. 22, 2022. 

ICE secured a final order of removal. He’ll stay in ICE detention pending local charges.

Our investigative team discovered over 2,000 files of child sex abuse material on Milfelt’s phone during a search of his vehicle.

tweets.text:
Wilfredo Jose Tortolero-Arriechi, a criminal illegal alien from Venezuela, stabbed 51-year-old Alberto Rangel inside Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital in San Francisco, Calif., Dec. 4. Sadly, Rangel died from the injuried two days later. Tortolero-Arriechi now faces charges for murder and use of a deadly weapon.

Tortolero-Arriechi's behavior grew increasingly alarming in the weeks prior to the attack. He allegedly threatened his own doctor and hospital staff.

ICE issued a detainer with the San Franciso County Jail, which asks for very basic cooperation: If local officials decide to release Tortolero-Arriechi for any reason, we need them to contact ICE so our officers can pick him up from the jail.

California sanctuary policies force local law enforcement to ignore ICE detainers and allow criminal illegal aliens — like Tortolero-Arriechi — to reenter the community illegally, putting Americans’ safety at risk.

tweets.text:
ICE arrested child sex offender Ricardo Nava-Daniel, of Mexico, during a targeted operation in Jackson, N.J., April 29.

Monmouth County convicted him of endangering a child with sexual conduct and sentenced him to 287 days in jail in 2015. Three years later, he pleaded guilty to DUI and driving without a liscense.

He applied for a green card AFTER his child endangerment conviction, but a judge ordered him deported in 2019. He was deported April 30, 2019, but reentered illegally at an unknown time.

ICE received a tip that he was living in Jackson last November, tracked him down, and will remove him as soon as possible.

tweets.text:
ICE Homeland Security Investigations Newark special agents, as part of the Homeland Security Task Force and in close partnership with our federal, state and local counterparts, announced a significant achievement April 23.

Our team made huge strides in combatting drug trafficking in New Jersey, as part of an ongoing investigation, with the seizure of:

➡️15 kilos of fentanyl
➡️3 kilos of cocaine
➡️19 firearms
➡️A 3D printer with ghost gun manufacturing capabilities

“Today’s operation marks a decisive step forward in our ongoing efforts to stem the flow of illicit opioids, secure our borders, and confront the fentanyl crisis that endangers lives across our state,” said ICE @HSINewark Special Agent in Charge Michael S. McCarthy. “New Jersey is safer as a result of these actions, and HSI Newark will continue to pursue those who threaten our communities with relentless determination.”

tweets.text:
ICE @HSI_DC special agents arrested Roni Mendez-Escobar during a targeted enforcement operation, April 22, on federal criminal charges of possessing and distributing CHILD SEX ABUSE MATERIAL. 

Mendez-Escobar is a previously deported criminal illegal from Guatemala who was charged with 15 counts of possessing CSAM in Fairfax County, Va., last year.

At that time, ICE lodged a detainer against him with Fairfax County, asking the local authorities to turn him over to ICE instead of releasing him back into the community.

Fairfax County refused our immigration detainer, released him and dismissed the charges last October.

He’s currently in U.S. Marshals’ custody awaiting prosecution on the new CSAM charges.

tweets.text:
James Andrew Wellesley, of the UK, will serve a 10-year prison sentence and must pay $1M in forfeiture, following a $100M Ponzi scheme involving a fake wine company. He was sentenced April 20.

Wellesley had two prior convictions in the U.K., both related to financial fraud. He was extradited to the U.S. last year to face justice for his investment fraud scheme.

Wellesley and his partner posed as executives at Bordeaux Cellars and raised nearly $100 million by promising investors regular interest payments from high net worth wine collectors. This scheme targeted nearly 140 individuals located around the world, including 71 people in the U.S., 21 in the U.K., and 10 in Hong Kong.

ICE HSI was proud to be a part of the FBI-led team that investigated this case.

Garcia Moran is facing charges of abduction of a person with intent to defile, sodomy by force or victim helplessness, and assault.

tweets.text:
When local authorities honor detainers, aliens can’t return to the community to reoffend and fewer ICE officers are needed.

Take Daniel Jesus Piedra-Fernandez, of Colombia, as an example:

👉He’s a violent aggravated felon with convictions for robbery, battery, burglary, property damage and domestic violence. 
👉He has a final order of removal and a recent arrest. 
👉ICE Salt Lake City took custody of him within the security of a local jail because officials there honored our detainer.

In a sanctuary jurisdiction, Piedra-Fernandez would have been released back into the community, and ICE would have to assemble a large team to apprehend him at-large.

tweets.text:
Hilda Esperanza Lopez-Hernandez fled the scene of a deadly accident while driving a red Ford Mustang in Fort Walton Beach, Fla., in 2020. Then, she called her boyfriend to switch vehicles with her.

The fatal hit-and-run incident should never have happened. She had no license, and she wasn’t supposed to be in this country. 

Lopez-Hernandez was convicted in Okaloosa County of hit-and-run, operating a motor vehicle without a license and hiding evidence Jan. 9, 2023. She was handed a four-year sentence.  

ICE Miami arrested her after she was released from jail and deported her April 12, 2026.

tweets.text:
The leader of a Massachusetts sex trafficking ring pleaded guilty to her role in sex trafficking two kids and one adult following an investigation by ICE @HSINewEngland.

Christy Parker, a U.S. citizen also known as Lula, used physical beatings, threats, intimidation, sleep deprivation, starvation and other means to coerce at least one adult victim and two child victims to engage in repeated commercial sex acts in and around Fall River. Although many Americans think ICE’s work is strictly immigration related, ICE enforces over 400 federal laws and statutes. Ending human trafficking is one of the agency’s most important missions.

tweets.text:
LATEST CONSPIRATOR SENTENCED IN 13-YEAR ICE HSI ILLICIT SOFTWARE PROBE

A Florida woman got 22 months in prison and a $50,000 fine March 2 for trafficking illicit certificate of authenticity labels that bear Microsoft software activation codes.

Heidi Richards was sentenced in a 13-year, nationwide ICE @HSIKansasCity-led probe into a trafficking network that collectively harvested and resold at a discount over 600,000 illicit key codes worth over $900 million.

The labels and codes made counterfeit software appear legitimate.

The investigation so far includes $20.6 million in seized assets, as well as arrests, indictments and convictions of nine major distributors. DOJ calls this the largest software prosecution in its history.

tweets.text:
ICE asked Long Island law enforcement to detain a Venezuelan national charged with attempted murder after allegedly trying to slash his billiards partner’s throat at Gilgo Beach February 28.

The Suffolk County DA alleged Ruben Guanipa Ramirez picked up the victim from his home, went to a pool hall, and eventually brought him to one of the most iconic murder sites in the state.

At Gilgo Beach, Guanipa Ramirez allegedly came up behind the victim, covered his mouth with a cloth, stabbed him and tried to slit his throat.

Ramirez was arrested later that same day in Nassau County. Investigators say his vehicle contained three kitchen knives, one which appeared covered in blood, rope, duct tape, blankets, plastic drop cloths, a large-wheeled bag, gloves and a boxcutter.

The suspect entered the U.S. in June 2019 on a B-2 tourist visa which required him to depart the U.S. by December 2019. Now, he faces attempted murder and assault charges in connection with the alleged incident.

Regardless of the result of the criminal case, he is not authorized to be in the United States.

ICE thanks Agnes Gibboney, Ronald’s mother, for her tremendous strength. ICE’s VOICE Office can never bring her son back, but it can be there to support her.
 
Angel families carry a heavy burden of loss and pain, knowing their lives were forever changed by an avoidable tragedy.
 
Thanks to President Trump, the VOICE Office is here to help them through it.

tweets.text:
ICE’s VOICE Office is a support system for victims and families of crimes linked to immigration. Family members, like Matthew Denice’s parents, know the value of working with the VOICE Office.
 
Matthew was killed by an illegal alien drunk driver — he died on the street at just 23.
 
While ICE officers and special agents work tirelessly to make the nation safer for Americans, ICE VOICE liaisons have a different mission.
 
VOICE victim liaisons:
 
—Help victims and their family members understand the immigration enforcement and removal process
—Provide access to social service professionals to refer victims to resources and service providers
—Help victims and their family members receive automated custody status information on criminal aliens

Toll free hotline: 855-48-VOICE, Monday-Friday 8 A.M. to 8 P.M. ET.

Celebrating the one-year anniversary of the Trump Administration's VOICE Office relaunch.

tweets.text:
HABITUAL BORDER INVADER & AGGRAVATED FELON ARRESTED

ICE San Antonio's Special Response Team and ICE's 287(g) partners arrested Jose Angel Reyes-Maldonado with a criminal warrant March 27. He's a previously convicted and deported Mexican criminal illegal alien.

He was deported in 2005 after serving seven years for shooting someone with a rifle.

Despite that, he continued reentering the U.S. illegally and evaded authorities for years.

He’ll face deportation again after facing federal illegal reentry and weapons charges for this arrest.

Special thanks to @CBP, the Gonzales County Sheriff’s Office and @NixonPolice.

Jose Luis Gomez, a serial criminal illegal alien, is charged with two counts of negligent homicide and DWI in connection with a crash that killed husband-and-wife motorcyclists, 70-year-old Michael Cordes and 67-year-old Freda Cordes, in Benton County, Arkansas.

ICE issued a detainer against Gomez with the Benton County detention facility March 27 to prevent him being released into the community.

tweets.text:
HOUSTON MAN GETS 179-YEAR SENTENCE IN ICE @HSI_HQ CHILD EXPLOITATION CASE

Samuel Rocky Munoz’s sentence came after @HSIHouston Corpus Christi and @InglesidePD discovered he transported a 13-year-old girl from Houston to his residence, gave her drugs and alcohol, and videotaped himself sexually assaulting her. 

Police initially encountered the child fleeing from Munoz’ home with a high blood alcohol content.

He was convicted of:
 
—Aggravated sexual assault of a child, 1st degree felony: 75 years and a $10,000 fine
—Promoting prostitution of a child under 18, 1st degree felony: 20 years
—Compelling prostitution of a child under 18, 1st degree felony: 20 years
—Sexual performance of a child under 14, 1st degree felony: 99 years
—Possession of CSAM/video, 2nd degree felony: 20 years
—Possession of child CSAM/video, 1st degree felony: 75 years and a $5,000 fine

The children of these Chinese nationals, Ann Mary Zheng and Alen Zheng, who were born in the U.S. after their parents illegally entered the country, are connected to the attempted IED attack at MacDill Air Force Base in Tampa, Florida.

Ann Mary Zheng was arrested by federal authorities upon her return from China. Authorities believe her brother Alen Zheng is still in China.

VIRGINIA PRESCHOOL EMPLOYEE CHARGED WITH MAKING CHILD SEX ABUSE MATERIAL

ICE @HSI_DC special agents were proud to assist on the investigation that led to charging Zachary Harger, a U.S. citizen of Rockingham County, VA, with three counts of MANUFACTURING CHILD SEX ABUSE MATERIAL and one count of UNLAWFUL FILMING.

According to local police, Harger was already on their radar following a complaint alleging inappropriate conduct with a child.

This investigation was a coordinated effort between ICE, the @SecretService, the Rockingham County Sheriff’s Office, @HarrisonburgVA Police Department and Child Protective Services. Harrisonburg City Public Schools provided vital assistance throughout the investigation.

DETAINED: CONVICTED MURDERER FROM VIETNAM

Minh Tieu is a criminal illegal alien from Vietnam convicted of MURDER Jan. 26, 1996, in Fort Wayne, IN. 

After serving over 25 years of his 45-year sentence in the slammer, he was released. Soon after, ICE placed Tieu on Alternatives to Detention.

Tieu violated ATD terms, so he was located by pinging his ankle monitor. ICE re-arrested him in March. 

He has a final order of removal, but ICE is waiting on Vietnam to issue travel documents to affect his deportation home.

tweets.text:
A pastor, a U.S. Air Force lieutenant colonel and a public school guard were among 14 arrested in a March 16-18 Ohio sex trafficking and child exploitation sting.

The arrests were part of an ongoing investigation by ICE @HSIDetroit and its partners into online ads offering sex with kids.

“These allegations are deeply disturbing, especially given that some of the accused held positions of public trust,” said ICE HSI Detroit Acting Special Agent in Charge Jared Murphey.

Arrested were:
-Timothy Fromholt
-William Miller
-Jordan Webster
-Tyler Heinze
-John Uxer
-Sean Cass
-Michael Sullivan
-Harrison Otuvedo
-Nolan Arthur
-Mark Duncan
-Sioeli Fakalata
-Mohamed Jumi 
-Travis Tatum
-Noah Blair

We appreciate our ongoing partnerships with the Butler Township Police Department, 
Miami Valley Human Trafficking Task Force, Montgomery County Sheriff’s Office and Oakwood Public Safety Department.

—Produced child sex abuse material

—Kept used little girls’ underwear with semen on it matching his own
—Possessed a baggie containing one victim’s hair

INTERNATIONAL FUGITIVE ARRESTED 

FRAMINGHAM, Mass. — ICE Boston arrested Jean Pierre Neves, a criminal illegal alien and FOREIGN FUGITIVE from Brazil, during targeted operations in Framingham March 11.

“Neves entered the United States illegally on June 10, 2021, amid the chaos of the Biden border crisis,” said ERO Boston acting Field Office Director David T. Wesling. “He was convicted in Brazil in September 2023 for illegally carrying a firearm. On Aug. 26, 2025, an international warrant was issued for his arrest.”  

Neves remains in ICE custody pending removal proceedings.

tweets.text:
Our ICE officers and agents are HEROES.

On March 25, 2026, an ICE agent helping to support @TSA operations at John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York jumped into action to help save the life of a one-year-old child experiencing a medical emergency.

The heroic actions of this officer began when a one-year-old child became unresponsive in the arms of his father, unable to breathe for almost two minutes. CCTV review shows a passenger in a TSA Precheck line holding his one-year-old in his arms when the child’s arms go lifeless; panic ensues, and the father is seen scrambling around the area, and calling for help.  

The agent working at his post, heard the screams from the father and other passengers and sprinted to the scene. The father handed the child to the officer, who then assessed the unresponsive child and began performing the Heimlich maneuver. After a few seconds, the child started breathing again. EMS personnel arrived on scene with medical equipment to further monitor and re-assess the child. The child was re-assessed and determined to be healthy enough to fly.

tweets.text:
Five Alabama defendants were sentenced to a combined nearly 120 years in prison March 19 in connection with a violent sex trafficking ring following an ICE @HSI_HQ investigation with Alabama law enforcement.

Ringleader Kimani Jones got 54 years, and his father Tremayne Lambert got 30 years for their roles in the extensive commercial sex operation that, for five years, forced women and girls to participate through violence and abuse.

The two were also ordered to pay $1,010,926.50 and $510,850 in restitution, respectively, to victims.

Others sentenced in the case:
—Joseph Keon Bowe: 19 ½+ years and $3,200 restitution
—Daryle Gardner: 16+ years and $5,000 restitution
—Aleecia Scott: 3 years and $1,000 restitution

Special thanks to the Alabama Law Enforcement Agency, Alabama Attorney General’s Office, Montgomery County Sheriff’s Office and Montgomery Police Department.

A fraudster from Togo with a lengthy criminal history picked up five more years in prison and was ordered to pay $5.7M in restitution and preliminary forfeiture after pleading guilty March 20 to conspiracy to commit money laundering and aggravated identity theft. 

Gedeon Mawulolo Agbeyome, a lawful permanent resident, used stolen identities to open shell businesses and bank accounts that received nearly $3 million in fraudulent funds, including money from a U.S. government environmental trust fund, in an international business email fraud scheme.

ICE @HSIMaryland special agents arrested him on a federal warrant. 

He also has an existing final removal order and a rap sheet that includes convictions and arrests for: 

—Aggravated ID theft
—Prostitution
—Counterfeit credit cards
—Commercial bribery/counterfeiting/forgery

tweets.text:
NEW ENGLAND SECURES PRISON SENTENCE FOR CALIFORNIA MAN IN COUNTERFEIT DRUG SCAM

Jimmy Fu, a U.S. citizen born in China living in West Hills, CA, landed himself in prison after an ICE @HSI_HQ probe into an India-based transnational criminal organization selling counterfeit drugs — including drugs containing fentanyl — via open net pharmaceutical websites.

During Fu’s sentencing March 19, he learned he’ll serve one year in prison and suffer the forfeiture of more than $689K. 

During the ICE @HSINewEngland investigation, at least 18 undercover purchases for prescription drugs were processed by Fu’s business, Axson Data, with each of the payments deposited into an Axson bank account. A review of all Axson’s bank accounts revealed thousands of checks deposited from individuals across the United States for various amounts of money ranging from hundreds to thousands of dollars. 

ICE HSI special agents have broad legal authority under federal law to conduct complex criminal investigations, including undercover operations and surveillance. Learn more about ICE HSI on our website.

ICE AGENTS SECURE 10-YEAR SENTENCE FOR SEX PREDATOR WHO TRIED TO MEET WITH TWO YOUNG KIDS

tweets.text:
ILLEGAL ALIEN CHARGED W/ATTEMPTED MURDER ARRESTED BY ICE AFTER PHILLY POLICE BROTHER BONDS HIM OUT
 
Previously convicted of a firearms offense in NY, Jamaican national Christopher Leon Bailey is now accused of running over a pedestrian in PA and fleeing the scene. 
 
Bailey, who overstayed a 2009 tourist visa, allegedly nearly hit two pedestrians crossing the road in January before chasing them down in a parking lot and nearly hitting them again. Kicked to the ground by the victim after exchanging words, Bailey allegedly pulled a knife on the two before chasing them down again with his car and running over the victim before fleeing the scene.
 
Bailey’s brother, a Philadelphia police officer, posted a cash bond Feb. 8, and the suspect was released without notification to ICE.  
 
ICE @EROPhiladelphia officers and @HSIPhiladelphia agents arrested him before he was remanded to the Delaware County Prison — in a sanctuary jurisdiction — March 16 when he appeared in court with his brother, who was in full uniform. Bailey will remain in ICE custody pending removal proceedings until the county issues a judicial writ.

His additional charges include:
 
—Felony aggravated assault: attempts to cause serious bodily injury or causes injury with extreme indifference
—Aggravated assault: attempts to cause serious bodily injury or causes injury with extreme indifference
—Aggravated assault: causes bodily injury with deadly weapon
—Recklessly endangering another person: possessing an instrument of crime
—Simple assault
—Disorderly conduct
—Reckless driving

ICE PROBE: OHIO CHILD SEX PREDATOR, TEACHER, GIRLS’ SOCCER COACH ENTICED CHILDREN TO SEND HIM EXPLICIT PHOTOS & SELF HARM

ICE @HSI_HQ discovered online communications between David Snell and a child living in CA, in which he enticed a child to make sexually explicit content and harm herself. 

Special agents also discovered that Snell, a U.S. citizen who previously worked as an educator for multiple schools and as a girls’ soccer coach and director of an organization that offers childcare services to underprivileged families, discussed his horrific fantasies of raping, torturing and murdering infants, children and adults.

This child sex predator groomed children to send him explicit and sexually degrading content — including depictions of the children self-harming.

The ICE @HSIDetroit investigation resulted in a 22.5-year sentence for Snell, who previously pleaded guilty to two counts of enticing a child to engage in criminal sexual conduct. HSI Detroit was assisted by other ICE field offices, including those in Burlington, Cincinnati, Monterey and New York. 

If you or someone you know has been victimized by Snell or another sexual predator, contact ICE HSI: 866-DHS-2-ICE

tweets.text:
ICE ARRESTS TENNESSEE THEFT SUSPECT WHO LED POLICE ON CHASE WITH KIDS IN THE CAR

ICE Nashville arrested Ramon Enrique Oliva Lao, a previously deported criminal illegal alien from Cuba who risked American lives by fleeing from local law enforcement with two small children in the car.

When local police officers in Spring Hill, TN, attempted to stop this criminal illegal alien suspected of shoplifting, he got in his vehicle and took off. Local authorities issued a be-on-the-lookout for the car.

Later that night he was spotted driving recklessly with the lights off. When police caught up to the vehicle, the suspect Lao pulled over then bolted into the woods — leaving his girlfriend and two young children in the car.

ICE arrested him March 11, 2026, after his release from the local jail following charges for evading arrest, child abuse and neglect, reckless endangerment and theft. 

Oliva Lao has a long history of criminal offenses, including multiple pending felony offenses and theft convictions from 2015 and 2025.

tweets.text:
WOMEN CONVICTED OF FELONY STALKING 

A jury found Coloradoan Ashleigh Brown and Californian Cynthia Raygoza guilty of felony stalking March 2 for following an ICE officer home in Los Angeles and livestreaming their pursuit on social media. 

Upon arriving at the victim’s neighborhood, the two shouted to bystanders while livestreaming on Instagram that their “neighbor is ICE,” “la migra lives here,” and “ICE lives on your street and you should know.” Raygoza also threatened to “pop” the victim.  

The victim’s wife and a concerned neighbor called 911 in response to the defendants’ actions. 

The victim’s wife – whom the defendants targeted with racial slurs – and children witnessed the incident and suffered emotional distress.   

The increased traffic from onlookers in the ensuing weeks caused the victim and his family to relocate to a different county. The forced move significantly disrupted the education of the victim’s children. The victim’s 3-year-old son, who has a disability, lost several social and health care benefits that were tied to his former county. 

ICE officers and special agents currently face an 8,000% increase in death threats and a 1,421% increase in assaults for merely doing the job Congress expects of them.

Deputy Lewis tragically died following the incident.

Sadly, Arguello-Acosta was granted Asylum in 2018. This is why @USCIS now reviews asylum cases more frequently — granting asylum cannot cost American lives!

He’ll face deportation proceedings after facing justice.

ICE HSI INVESTIGATION LEADS TO 5-YEAR SENTENCE IN CHILD SEXUAL ABUSE MATERIAL CASE 

ICE @HSI_HQ caught Ricardo Velazquez Reyes, of Pasco, WA, distributing files on multiple occasions that showed kids, some under 12 years old, being sexually abused.  

This material often depicts the worst day of a young child’s life, yet Velazquez Reyes distributed these horrific images and videos — gaining personal gratification from them.  

He began his five-year prison sentence March 10, to be followed by 15–years of supervised release. 

ICE HSI relentlessly pursues child predators to bring them to justice.

tweets.text:
MICHIGAN ORTHODONTIST GETS 60 YEARS FOR SERIAL CHILD EXPLOITATION CRIMES AFTER ICE PROBE

U.S. citizen Thomas Shannon, 44, was sentenced for exploiting numerous 12- to 17-year-old children online between 2019 and 2024.

Police discovered his crimes when a NC 16-year-old reported being sexually exploited on Snapchat. 

ICE @HSI_HQ investigators identified the perpetrator as Shannon, then an orthodontist in Grandville. HSI special agents found at least 16 other child victims, one of whom was only 12, on Shannon’s account.

Another child victim Shannon met and exploited through a different app came forward while he was already on bond for child sexual abuse material charges. 

His yacht, where he committed these crimes, was forfeited as part of the prosecution.

Felix Domingo Perez Camacho, a criminal illegal alien from Guatemala, was taken into custody by ICE this week after he caused a motor vehicle accident in Georgia, killing 63-year-old Thomas Wayne Reynolds, a beloved father to five children and grandfather of 10.

ERO officers located and arrested Perez Camacho within 24 hours of initiating the search for him.

Perez Camacho has a prior criminal history that includes multiple traffic-related convictions, as well as a conviction for hit and run. He entered the United States at an unknown time, in an unknown place, evading detection and apprehension.

He remains in ICE custody pending further proceedings. U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement is committed to keeping illegal aliens out of American communities, and out of our country.

tweets.text:
Brothers Bhaskar and Arun Savani, who ran a visa fraud scheme and built a multimillion-dollar criminal enterprise, face prison sentences of over 400 years after their convictions this month thanks to a multiagency investigation that included ICE @HSI_HQ. 

The Savani brothers:  

—Filed false H-1B visa applications and petitions to exploit foreign workers who were dependent on the Savani Group 

—Defrauded Medicaid of more than $30M 

—Conspired to place prototype dental implants not cleared by the FDA in human patients without their consent 

—Laundered money through a complex web of Savani Group corporate entities’ bank accounts 

—Fraudulently claimed personal expenses as business expenses and failed to pay taxes 

“Today’s convictions send a clear message that those who build criminal enterprises on the backs of vulnerable patients, exploited workers, and U.S. taxpayers will be held to account,” said ICE @HSIPhiladelphia Special Agent in Charge Eric McLoughlin.

tweets.text:
MASSACHUSETTES MAN INTENDING TO HAVE SEX WITH 14-YEAR-OLD GETS 7 YEARS AFTER ICE HSI STING

43-year-old U.S. citizen Arthur Picanco was sentenced March 9 for traveling to New Hampshire to have sex with a “minor” in 2024 after seeing an online ad placed by HSI New England special agents that appeared to depict underage girls.

Picanco unknowingly coordinated with an undercover agent to meet at a hotel. Manchester police arrested him after he arrived with cash and a condom and confirmed he wanted to go up to the hotel room to meet a 14-year-old girl.

The two-day operation led to six arrests, including a NH schoolteacher. Each defendant met with an undercover agent and attempted to sex traffic one of two fictitious children prior to their arrests.

tweets.text:
TWO AMERICANS GET 20 YEARS EACH IN ICE HSI-LED FENTANYL PILL PRESS PROBE 

Washington state residents Nicolas Bryan Adams and Timothy Maddox were convicted of fentanyl distribution and unlawful mail after an ICE HSI Spokane-led investigation into a conspiracy to mass produce and distribute fentanyl-laced pills using a commercial grade pill press and dies from China.  

The two set up a pill press operation in the basement of Adams’ residence, mixing powdered fentanyl and cutting agents to make their own fentanyl pills for bulk distribution into the community. 

When arrested, the two had enough fentanyl powder for 2 MILLION fentanyl-laced pills. 

They also had: 

—Meth, cocaine, heroin & MDMA 
—Assault rifles, fully automatic machine guns and body armor. 

Special thanks to ICE @HSIElPaso and our DEA, USPSIS and Spokane County Sheriff’s Office partners. 

@HSI_HQ

A jury convicted 65-year-old Ramon Arellano Sandoval after an ICE @HSIMiami probe found he exchanged thousands of text and video messages with a 14-year-old girl in rural Florida.

Knowing the victim’s age, Sandoval repeatedly solicited sexually explicit videos from her and directed her to produce child sexual abuse material, often in exchange for electronic payments.

He also traveled from Tennessee to Florida to engage in commercial sex with the minor.

@HSI_HQ

tweets.text:
FLORIDA HIGH SCHOOL TEACHER SENTENCED FOR GIVING FIREARMS TO TRANSNATIONAL CRIME RING

Shannon Nicole Samlalsingh, a U.S. citizen and former Hillsborough County high school teacher, will serve prison time for conspiring to provide firearms to a Trinidad-based transnational criminal organization. 

An ICE @HSITampa and @ATFHQ investigation revealed that she falsely stated on ATF forms that several firearms she was about to purchase were for her. Yet, after buying the weapons, she gave them to transnational criminal organization members — who smuggled them into Trinidad.

@HSI_HQ

tweets.text:
ICE NEWARK INVESTIGATION LEADS TO BRITISH CHILD SEX PREDATOR’S PRISON SENTENCE

Stephen Husdon, of the UK, began a 140-month sentence March 3 after ICE HSI Newark busted him for arranging to cross state lines into New Jersey to sexually abuse two girls.

He unknowingly provided an undercover law enforcement agent $1,200 in cash hoping to have illicit sex with the minors. 

When ICE special agents searched his laptop, they found 1,751 images of child sex abuse, including images of prepubescent children, toddlers and infants being sexually abused.

tweets.text:
ICE ARRESTS TdA TERRORIST AT NEWARK LIBERTY INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT 
 
ICE @HSINewark arrested Jeandrik Gabriel Graterol Reyes, a criminal illegal alien from Venezuela and confirmed member of Tren de Aragua — a designated terrorist organization — during a targeted enforcement operation at Newark Liberty International Airport March 8. 
 
Graterol Reyes’ criminal history includes a cocaine-related conviction. He’ll stay in ICE custody pending removal proceedings. 
  
ICE HSI’s Newark Airport Group initiated the investigation after getting information that the alien was on an inbound domestic flight from Orlando. 
  
ICE HSI received critical assistance from ICE ERO Newark’s Fugitive Operations, ICE’s Office of the Principal Legal Advisor and TSA’s Federal Air Marshal Service.

tweets.text:
CRIMINAL ILLEGAL ALIEN ALLEGEDLY MURDERED HIS 3-YEAR-OLD NEPHEW, DETAINER LODGED

ICE has lodged an immigration detainer against Samuel Antonio Maldonado-Erazo, a criminal illegal alien accused of violently abusing and then murdering his three-year-old nephew.

Maldonado-Erazo’s sister, Wendy Esther Hernandez Reyes, was the little boy’s mother; she was removed from the U.S. Jan. 26 and chose not to take her U.S. citizen child with her.

Escambia County Sheriff Chip Simmons said the child’s injuries included at least 17 strikes to the head, extensive bruising, intentional burns, a broken collarbone, a transected pancreas from blunt trauma, and several broken ribs — one of which was completely detached from his spine.

tweets.text:
ICE HSI INVESTIGATION LEADS TO ALLEGED CHILD RAPIST’S ARREST IN LOUISIANA

ICE HSI’s law enforcement partners in Louisiana arrested James Michael Hill, a U.S. citizen, at his home in Hammond, March 4 for allegedly RAPING, MOLESTING and SEXUALLY ABUSING A CHILD UNDER 13 and creating child sexual abuse material.

This arrest was a result of a joint investigation with ICE HSI, the Louisiana Bureau of Investigation’s Cyber Crime Unit and the Hammond Police Department.

This investigation is ongoing. 

ICE special agents work tirelessly to protect children from sexual predators —including sexual predators who are U.S. citizens — through its HSI directorate.

If you have any information or were victimized by this man, please contact the toll-free ICE Tip Line: 866-347-2423.

tweets.text:
“SHOOT THEM ALL” — MAN WHO THREATENED ICE AGENTS, NC POLICE PAYS THE PRICE
 
Johnathan Trent Thomas was sentenced to 15 months in prison after threatening to murder federal officers and Charlotte Mecklenburg PD involved in immigration enforcement.
 
Thomas called @CMPD multiple times in May 2025 claiming to be wearing body armor and armed with high-caliber weapons looking to target ICE agents or plain clothed officers in Charlotte using armor-piercing ammunition and night vision devices. He threatened to “shoot them all” if he saw anyone enforcing immigration law.
 
Thomas also claimed to have Tannerite explosive all around his house and “could do a whole lot better than” April 29, 2024 — the day four law enforcement officers were killed in the line of duty in Charlotte.
 
Undeterred, an ICE @HSI_HQ special response team arrested him at his Linwood home and seized three rifles, gas masks, a handgun and a variety of ammunition.
 
ICE @HSI_Charlotte investigated the case with help from its partners at the @ATFHQ, Davidson County Sheriff’s Office, @TownofWaxhawNC Police Department and CMDP.

tweets.text:
New York rap artist Jimmy Francois aka Bentley Bugz was arraigned on a six-count CHILD SEX TRAFFICKING indictment March 6 following a multiagency investigation that included ICE HSI.

Francois allegedly sex trafficked a 16-year-old girl throughout multiple states — forcing her into prostitution and creating child sexual abuse material of his victim, which he posted online — and faces other charges.

Investigators uncovered a video in which he allegedly assaulted, demeaned and threatened the 16-year-old using statements such as, “I will personally break your bones.”

"The defendant's exploitation and trafficking of an innocent teenager can only be described as depravity in its most vile form," said ICE HSI New York Special Agent in Charge Michael Alfonso.

tweets.text:
Akira Salinas-Ruiz, of Mexico, MURDERED HIS GIRLFRIEND’S TODDLER in 2006. The following year, he was sentenced to 20 years in prison after pleading no contest.

The prison handed the criminal illegal alien to ICE when he completed his sentence Feb. 17, rather than release him. He had a 2008 removal order, so ICE promptly deported him.

The toddler’s tragic death was avoidable. 

Salinas-Ruiz never should have been in the country in the first place, given that he snuck into the U.S. along Arizona’s border near Sonora, Mexico, in 2001 and was granted voluntary return in 2002.

At the scene, paramedics were told that the very small boy fell and was having trouble breathing, but a doctor concluded the toddler’s injuries, which included bruises on his neck and stomach, were consistent with shaken baby syndrome.

tweets.text:
ACCUSED CARTEL NARCOTERRORIST AND ICE MOST WANTED ARRESTED BY HSI & PARTNERS IN MEXICO
 
Mexican national Roberto “Beto” BAZAN-Salinas -- associated with the Cartel del Golfo and wanted for his role in smuggling multi kilogram shipments of cocaine, methamphetamine and marijuana into the U.S. – was arrested by ICE HSI and its partners yesterday in Salamanca, Guanajuato, Mexico.
 
HSI Monterrey, HSI Matamoros, HSI Beaumont and the Fiscalia General de la Republica Interpol coordinated the arrest as part of an ongoing HSI-led investigation into the CDG Foreign Terrorist Organization.
 
Special thanks to our other partners at the DEA, Corpus Christi Police Department and Texas Department of Public Safety.
 
Stay tuned to our newsroom for more details.

tweets.text:
COLD CASE MURDERER DEPORTED

In 2009, Kemar Hamilton, a Jamaican criminal illegal alien, murdered a 58-year-old Jamaican immigrant father of seven in Bronx, NY — the victim had been shot outside his own home during a mugging.

Hamilton fled the scene.

While Hamilton hid from justice on New York streets, immigration officials were already processing his removal orders, and an immigration judge ordered him removed in 2009.

The case went cold until 2011, when Hamilton was arrested during a NYPD traffic stop for illegally carrying a loaded handgun. 

The following year, investigators determined that Hamilton was a DNA match for evidence collected at the murder scene.

His other convictions include:
— Aggravated assault
— Smuggling contraband into a prison
— Attempted assault
— Criminal weapons possession

A judge in New York sentenced Hamilton to over a decade in prison. 

ICE @EROBuffalo officers arrested him, Oct. 6, 2025, upon his release from the Wende Correctional Facility in Alden to prevent his release into the community. 

ICE @ERONewOrleans deported him last month.

ILLEGAL LIBERIAN NATIONAL DESERTED PENNSYLVANIA NATIONAL GUARD PRIOR TO ICE ARREST WHILE EMPLOYED AS MINNESOTA CORRECTIONS OFFICER 
 
Ending a 12-year run of immigration violations and fraud, ICE St. Paul officers arrested criminal illegal alien Morris Brown, who was working as a MN corrections officer after falsely claiming U.S. citizenship, Jan. 15. 
 
Brown entered the U.S. on a student visa in 2014 but fraudulently joined the PA Army National Guard instead of going to school despite having no legal status — then he deserted! 
 
• 2015 – Student visa revoked 
• 2020 – Green card denied for failure to disclose military service and false U.S. citizenship claims 
• 2022 – Caught and discharged by the Guard 
• 2024 – Applied to naturalize based on nondisclosure of fraudulent military service 
 
He faces removal and potential prosecution for his crimes.

tweets.text:
CHILD SEXUAL PREDATOR DENATURALIZED 

On Feb. 9, Isidro Arcenio Alvarado was convicted of naturalization fraud. A federal judge sentenced him to six months in prison, revoked his U.S. citizenship, and issued a judicial removal order to ensure his immediate deportation as soon as his sentence is complete.   

The Salvadoran national took his oath of citizenship Oct. 12, 2022, but he lied during his naturalization interview by denying his past crimes — repeatedly sexually molesting a 10-year-old child from January 2019 through April 2021. 

Alvarado wasn’t arrested until after he naturalized, so immigration officials were unaware of his crimes prior to granting citizenship. 

Through Operation False Haven, ICE finds, prosecutes and removes convicted felons who lie on immigration forms.

tweets.text:
ICE HSI-LED INVESTIGATION PUTS TEXAS CHILD SEX PREDATOR BEHIND BARS FOR 23 YEARS 

Walter Wilkins, 24, of Weslaco, TX, was sentenced in January to 23 years in prison for sexually extorting kids by luring them with vape pens. 

The investigation began when the mother of a 13-year-old girl reported that her child had been sexually assaulted. 

Wilkins lured the girl and sexually exploited her in exchange for vape pens when he was out on bond and facing prosecution for two other cases of sexually abusing children. He picked her up in a white SUV and sexually assaulted her and videotaped the incident. 

He also blackmailed her — telling her that he would show the child sexual abuse material he created of her to her classmates if she refused to participate in further abuse. 

Another girl told police that Wilkins offered to sell her drugs, but when she couldn’t pay, he sexually assaulted her. 

“Wilkins’ actions represent the worst kind of predatory behavior, targeting and exploiting vulnerable children in our community,” said acting Special Agent in Charge John A. Pasciucco, who heads the ICE HSI San Antonio field office. “HSI, together with our law enforcement partners, worked tirelessly to uncover the full scope of Wilkins’ crimes and ensure he faces justice.” 

The ICE HSI Child Exploitation Task Force, with assistance from the Weslaco Police Department and Hidalgo County Sheriff’s Office, determined that Wilkins sexually exploited or abused at least four children in all.  

If you or someone you know has been sexually exploited by an online predator, call 866-DHS-2-ICE.

14 “BLACK GANGSTER DISCIPLES” INDICTED FOR DRUG & GUN TRAFFICKING IN TACOMA, WA

An ICE @HSISeattle-led wiretap investigation led to the gang members’ arrests, including ring-leader Kevin Salgado, who’s accused of leading the group to distribute methamphetamine, fentanyl, cocaine, and counterfeit oxycodone and Xanax pills across the country.

—10.5 kg of counterfeit oxycodone pills
—887.9 g of fentanyl pills
—924 g of fentanyl powder
—557.6 g of cocaine
—268.8 g of Xanax pills

VIOLENT CRIMINAL ILLEGAL ALIEN RAMMED ICE VEHICLE & LED RECKLESS VEHICLE PURSUIT

ICE Boston, in cooperation with the DEA and ATF Boston, arrested John Reino-Tello, a criminal illegal alien from Ecuador, in Connecticut Jan. 20.

Reino, a priority target due to his criminal history, tried to evade arrest by violently and intentionally ramming an ICE vehicle. 
 
Without regard for the safety of law enforcement, himself or the public at large, Reino then led officers and agents on a reckless vehicle pursuit — eventually leaving his vehicle in drive and fleeing on foot. 

ICE safely apprehended him despite his continued resistance.

His criminal history includes the following charges:
—2 counts of risk of injury to a child
—Criminal violation of a restraining order 
—Assault
—Violation of a protection order

tweets.text:
CARTEL-BUILT TUNNELS BURIED
 
Oscar Ivan Carillo of Texas was sentenced for aiding narco-terrorist cartels in conducting smuggling operations through man-made tunnels.
 
A powered and ventilated tunnel constructed by the international drug cartel La Nueva Empresa ran beneath the Rio Grande, linking Ciudad Juarez, Mexico, to El Paso, Texas. 

An ICE @HSIElPaso operation, in partnership with U.S. Border Patrol, resulted in the arrests of additional key members of the organization and the seizure of tunnel construction tools and building materials.
 
Illegal aliens guided through the tunnel into the U.S. exited a manhole cover undetected into a box truck modified with a trap door.

ILLEGAL ALIEN TRUCK DRIVER CHARGED WITH KILLING 4 IN INDIANA

On February 3rd, criminal illegal alien Bekzhan Beishekeev of Kyrgyzstan allegedly swerved and drove his eighteen-wheeler head-on into a van killing four innocent Americans: 50-year-old Henry Eicher, 25-year-old Menno Eicher, 19-year-old Paul Eicher and 23-year-old Simon Girod.

Beishekeev entered the U.S. using the Biden admin's disastrous CBP One App and was issued his CDL by Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro's Department of Transportation.

ICE's 287(g) partners at the Indiana State Police arrested Beishekeev, and he'll remain in ICE Fort Wayne's custody pending immigration proceedings.

This tragedy and loss of American lives could have been prevented had PA not issued a CDL to an illegal alien.

tweets.text:
ARMED ROBBERY & DRUG TRAFFICKING  

ICE Milwaukee officers arrested Vietnamese criminal illegal alien Giang Van “Shorty” Doan Jan. 9 after a 14-year sentence for two counts of possessing methamphetamine with intent to distribute in CA.  

The “Tiny Oriental Crips” gang member was arrested and charged in a massive 2014 San Diego gang-related gun- and methamphetamine-trafficking bust by ICE Homeland Security Investigations that also tied to the Oriental Killer Boys, Oriental Mob Crips, Viet Boys, Logan Heights Calle Treinta and Linda Vista Crips gangs.  

Originally granted legal permanent residence status as a refugee in 1982, Doan instead turned to crime early and was ordered deported in 2002 after a 1995 nine-year armed robbery sentence. After his release, he continued victimizing Californians, garnering four years for amphetamine sales in 2007.  

This doesn't happen under this administration — we use third-country removals and will ensure this is the last time he sees U.S. soil.

tweets.text:
ICE HSI INVESTIGATION LANDS HUMAN SMUGGLER BEHIND BARS  

ICE Homeland Security Investigations broke up a deadly human smuggling operation that trafficked over 500 people and tragically cost the lives of many. 

A federal judge has now sentenced the mastermind behind the ring, Erica Aracely Carmona, to 11 years in prison and ordered her to pay nearly a million dollars for her heinous crimes. 

Special agents discovered she purchased a trailer to transport illegal aliens, in a scheme that generated over nearly $5 million in illicit gains in just a year and a half.   

“The loss of life in this case is a stark reminder that human smuggling is not just a crime — it is a deadly threat to our communities,” said HSI San Antonio acting Special Agent in Charge John A. Pasciucco. “Carmona’s sentencing demonstrates that HSI and our partners will relentlessly pursue and dismantle criminal organizations that prioritize profit over human life.”

tweets.text:
ICE-HSI INVESTIGATION LEADS TO GUILTY PLEA FROM FORMER DES MOINES SUPERINTENDENT  

Ian Andre Roberts, a criminal illegal alien who was hired by Des Moines Public Schools as a superintendent, pleaded guilty Jan. 22 to federal charges following an investigation led by ICE Homeland Security Investigations.  

Roberts falsely claimed to be a U.S. citizen on employment paperwork related to his position at Des Moines Public Schools in June 2023 — he has never been a U.S. citizen.  

When ICE arrested him on Sept. 26, 2025, he knowingly carried a loaded Glock pistol in his vehicle even though he was unlawfully present in the county. He also had three more firearms at his home including a loaded rifle, a loaded pistol and a shotgun. It is a federal crime for illegal aliens to possess firearms.

tweets.text:
Elaine Antao and Manal Wasef — both of Brooklyn, NY — pleaded guilty Jan. 15 in federal court to conspiring to defraud Medicaid by paying health care kickbacks for services that were not provided at two Brooklyn adult day cares and a home health care company. 

When sentenced, the defendants each face up to 10 years in prison.  

ICE HSI New York special agents were part of the federal team investigating this fraud case. 

“These defendants placed profit over people and public well-being and stole $68 million in welfare funds meant for those who need it most,” said ICE New York Special Agent in Charge Ricky J. Patel.

ICE HSI New York special agents were part of the federal team investigating this fraud case. 

“These defendants placed profit over people and public well-being and stole $68 million in welfare funds meant for those who need it most,” said ICE New York Special Agent in Charge Ricky J. Patel.

tweets.text:
On January 21, ICE Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) responded to a request from the Sherburne County Sheriff’s Office in Zimmerman, MN, to help locate a missing 7-year-old girl after an AMBER Alert. HSI teams braved sub-freezing temperatures, canvassed neighborhoods, and worked closely with local law enforcement, gathering surveillance information to aid the search.
 
On January 22, HSI was notified that the child was found safe and the alleged sexual assaulter and kidnapper, 28-year-old Joseph Andrew Bragg, was taken into custody.
 
HSI is proud to have helped bring this young girl home and ensure alleged child predators are removed from Minnesota’s streets.

tweets.text:
NEW INDICTMENTS IN TdA ATM JACKPOTTING INVESTIGATION 
 
A federal grand jury returned a new indictment charging 31 people for their roles in a multi-million-dollar ATM jackpotting conspiracy. 
 
The additional defendants bring the total number of charged defendants to 87. 
 
Charges include: 
 
— CONSPIRACY TO COMMIT BANK FRAUD 
— CONSPIRACY TO COMMIT BANK BURGLARY & COMPUTER FRAUD 
— BANK BURGLARY
 
Members of Tren de Aragua, a designated foreign terrorist organization, use ATM jackpotting crimes committed across America to fund the organization and its members’ abhorrent crimes, including HUMAN TRAFFICKING, CHILD SEX TRAFFICKING, KIDNAPPING and MURDER.  
 
ICE Homeland Security Investigations Omaha and the @FBI’s Omaha field office are investigating this case with help from federal, state and local law enforcement across the country.

tweets.text:
MS-13 TERRORIST CHARGED WITH HOMICIDE, ASSAULT & MORE 

ICE officers arrested Salvadoran criminal illegal alien William Geoevani Martinez-Vivas after he served time for illegal reentry. 

He’s an active member of the MS-13 gang and was charged in Santa Ana, CA, with homicide, assault, burglary, trespassing and property crimes. 

Because he served time in federal custody, we didn’t have to gather a huge team of officers to arrest him in the community — we just picked him up from a secure facility. 

This is what it looks like when states work with us instead of against us. Minnesota should take note.
